images.caption: Figure 1:EVA-Bench framework overview.The simulation orchestrates parallel per-scenario bot-to-bot audio sessions over WebSocket in which the User Simulator — configured with a scenario-specific goal, persona, and conversational TTS voice — interacts with the Voice Agent under test. The Tool Executor handles all agent tool calls deterministically. Completed conversations pass throughSimulator Validationthat trigger automatic regeneration on failure before entering theQuality Measurementsphase, which producesEVA-AandEVA-Xpass@1, pass@k, and pass^k scores in addition toDiagnosticmetrics.

Figure 3:Perturbation effect onTurn-Takingpooled across domains. Bars show the mean delta from clean trials; 95% bootstrap (CIs) on the per-scenario delta. Bar colors encode the perturbation condition:■\blacksquareaccent,■\blacksquarebackground noise,■\blacksquareaccent + background noise. Asterisks mark cells significant after Holm-Bonferroni correction (*p<0.05p<0.05,**p<0.01p<0.01,***p<0.001p<0.001). Models listed in AppendixG.

Figure 4:Sensitivity of EVA-X pass@1 to the turn-taking pass-thresholdτtt\tau_{\text{tt}}. We sweepτtt\tau_{\text{tt}}from0.500.50to0.950.95in0.050.05increments and recompute EVA-X pass@1 at each value, holding the Conversation Progression and Conciseness thresholds fixed at0.50.5. The dashed vertical line marks the pass threshold valueτtt=0.8\tau_{\text{tt}}=0.8. (a) Architecture-aggregated. Each curve is the mean per-scenario EVA-X pass@1 (averaged overk=5k=5trials per scenario), pooled with equal weight across all (system, scenario) pairs within an architecture class (cascade, hybrid, S2S); shaded bands are percentile bootstrap95%95\%CIs from1,0001{,}000resamples of (system, scenario) pairs. (b) Per-system. One curve per benchmarked system, colored by architecture; shaded bands are bootstrap95%95\%CIs from1,0001{,}000resamples of scenarios within the system. Architecture ordering (S2S > cascade > hybrid) is preserved at every threshold; per-system rankings flip only between close competitors.

Figure 5:Mean transcription accuracy on key entities per cascade STT model,
pooled across the three EVA-Bench domains. Each bar is the mean of per-scenario
accuracies (one value per scenario, averaged overk=5k{=}5trials);
whiskers are 95% normal-approximation CIs on that mean. Models are sorted
ascending. Nova-3 aggregates the two cascades that share it
(Nova-3 + GPT-5.4 + Sonic 3 and Nova-3 + GPT-5.4-mini + Aura-2),
giving it twice as many scenario observations and a correspondingly tighter
interval. Scribe-v2.2-Realtime saturates near1.01.0on this benchmark; the gap to
the next-best STT (Parakeet-1.1) exceeds0.190.19.
Figure 6:Mean key entity transcription accuracy and mean task completion correlation across the 7 evaluated cascade systems.

Figure 7:Per-pipeline-type means on the four facets, computed on clean runs only. Error bars are 95% percentile bootstrap intervals over systems-within-type, shown as descriptive uncertainty bands; faint dots are per-system means. Hybrid (n=2n=2) is shown as individual system points only.

Figure 8:Perturbation effect onEVA-A pass@1across all evaluated systems, pooled across the three EVA domains. Bars show the mean delta from clean trials (negative = drop under perturbation); whiskers are 95% percentile bootstrap CIs on the per-scenario delta. Bar colors encode the perturbation condition:■\blacksquareaccent,■\blacksquarebackground noise,■\blacksquareaccent + background noise. Asterisks mark cells significant after Holm-Bonferroni correction within each model (*p<0.05p<0.05,**p<0.01p<0.01,***p<0.001p<0.001). Models, left to right:Cascade: Cohere + Gemma-4-26B + Voxtral, ElevenAgents (Scribe + Gemini-3-Flash  + Conversational v3), Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4-mini + Aura, Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro, Whisper + Qwen3.5-27B + Voxtral;Hybrid: Gemini-3-Flash + Gemini-3.1-Flash, Ultravox;S2S: Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live, GPT-Realtime-1.5, GPT-Realtime-mini.

Figure 9:Perturbation effect onEVA-X pass@1across all evaluated systems, pooled across the three EVA domains. Bars show the mean delta from clean trials (negative = drop under perturbation); whiskers are 95% percentile bootstrap CIs on the per-scenario delta. Bar colors encode the perturbation condition:■\blacksquareaccent,■\blacksquarebackground noise,■\blacksquareaccent + background noise. Asterisks mark cells significant after Holm-Bonferroni correction within each model (*p<0.05p<0.05,**p<0.01p<0.01,***p<0.001p<0.001). Models, left to right:Cascade: Cohere + Gemma-4-26B + Voxtral, ElevenAgents (Scribe + Gemini-3-Flash  + Conversational v3), Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4-mini + Aura, Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro, Whisper + Qwen3.5-27B + Voxtral;Hybrid: Gemini-3-Flash + Gemini-3.1-Flash, Ultravox;S2S: Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live, GPT-Realtime-1.5, GPT-Realtime-mini.

Figure 10:Perturbation effect onTask Completionacross all evaluated systems, pooled across the three EVA domains. Bars show the mean delta from clean trials (negative = drop under perturbation); whiskers are 95% percentile bootstrap CIs on the per-scenario delta. Bar colors encode the perturbation condition:■\blacksquareaccent,■\blacksquarebackground noise,■\blacksquareaccent + background noise. Asterisks mark cells significant after Holm-Bonferroni correction within each model (*p<0.05p<0.05,**p<0.01p<0.01,***p<0.001p<0.001). Models, left to right:Cascade: Cohere + Gemma-4-26B + Voxtral, ElevenAgents (Scribe + Gemini-3-Flash  + Conversational v3), Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4-mini + Aura, Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro, Whisper + Qwen3.5-27B + Voxtral;Hybrid: Gemini-3-Flash + Gemini-3.1-Flash, Ultravox;S2S: Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live, GPT-Realtime-1.5, GPT-Realtime-mini.

Figure 11:Perturbation effect onTranscription Accuracy (Key Entities)for cascade systems, pooled across the three EVA domains. Bars show the mean delta from clean trials (negative = drop under perturbation); whiskers are 95% percentile bootstrap CIs on the per-scenario delta. Bar colors encode the perturbation condition:■\blacksquareaccent,■\blacksquarebackground noise,■\blacksquareaccent + background noise. Asterisks mark cells significant after Holm-Bonferroni correction within each model (*p<0.05p<0.05,**p<0.01p<0.01,***p<0.001p<0.001). Models, left to right:Cascade: Cohere + Gemma-4-26B + Voxtral, ElevenAgents (Scribe + Gemini-3-Flash  + Conversational v3), Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4-mini + Aura, Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro, Whisper + Qwen3.5-27B + Voxtral;Hybrid: Gemini-3-Flash + Gemini-3.1-Flash, Ultravox;S2S: Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live, GPT-Realtime-1.5, GPT-Realtime-mini.

Figure 12:Perturbation effect onConversation Progressionacross all evaluated systems, pooled across the three EVA domains. Bars show the mean delta from clean trials (negative = drop under perturbation); whiskers are 95% percentile bootstrap CIs on the per-scenario delta. Bar colors encode the perturbation condition:■\blacksquareaccent,■\blacksquarebackground noise,■\blacksquareaccent + background noise. Asterisks mark cells significant after Holm-Bonferroni correction within each model (*p<0.05p<0.05,**p<0.01p<0.01,***p<0.001p<0.001). Models, left to right:Cascade: Cohere + Gemma-4-26B + Voxtral, ElevenAgents (Scribe + Gemini-3-Flash  + Conversational v3), Ink + Haiku-4.5 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4 + Sonic, Nova + GPT-5.4-mini + Aura, Parakeet + Gemma-4-31B + Kokoro, Whisper + Qwen3.5-27B + Voxtral;Hybrid: Gemini-3-Flash + Gemini-3.1-Flash, Ultravox;S2S: Gemini-3.1-Flash-Live, GPT-Realtime-1.5, GPT-Realtime-mini.

countkk, one panel per metric. Each line is one of the 12 evaluated
